div.h3: Phase 3: Recursive Rewrite Logic Implementation Details
div.p: 1. Fixed-Point Iteration (Convergence) The RuleEngine continues applying the set of rules until no rule reports a changed=true status. This allows cascading optimizations (e.g., Constant Folding enabling a Predicate Pushdown).
2. Pattern Matching Protocol Rules must be idempotent. The pattern match Filter(Join(L, R)) is checked via type-erasure safe visitors or NodeType enum checks.

3. Recursive Descent (Bottom-Up) Rule application must be recursive. By calling apply(node.child) before evaluating the current node, we ensure that deep nested subqueries are simplified before the parent attempts to re-route predicates.
4. Schema Safety (M3.4) The SchemaResolver is critical. A filter can only be pushed into a Join branch if all identifiers referenced in the expression are present in that branch's output schema.

g.title: The main Fixed-Point Iteration Controller (C++17 Singleton)
Validates column availability in subtrees via Catalog lookup

g.text: can_push(Expr* e, const Schema& s): bool
